extracted_items.content: Architecture: modular monolith. Extract to microservices only when there is clear scaling need justifying the complexity.
API: GraphQL with Apollo Server. Chosen to support multiple clients (web, mobile, third-party integrations) with complex nested query requirements.
Authentication: OAuth2 with PKCE for all clients. JWT tokens using RS256 algorithm. No API key authentication for first-party clients.
Search infrastructure: Elasticsearch. Chosen for complex full-text search requirements; TypeSense deemed too limited.
Caching: Redis for session cache and hot data. TTL configuration: 1 hour default for most keys, 24 hours for user profiles.
Background jobs: Celery with Redis broker. Periodic tasks via Celery Beat. No separate job service to avoid additional service boundary.
File storage: S3-compatible storage using Cloudflare R2. CDN via Cloudflare. AWS S3 explicitly rejected due to egress costs.
Observability: Structured JSON logs shipped to Grafana Loki. Distributed traces via OpenTelemetry to Grafana Tempo. ELK stack rejected as too heavy.
Infrastructure as Code: Terraform for all cloud resources. Manual console changes are prohibited — all infrastructure must be declared in IaC to prevent drift.
Secrets management: HashiCorp Vault for all secrets. No secrets allowed in environment variables directly; all must flow through Vault. This is non-negotiable.
Error tracking: Sentry for error tracking and monitoring across all services.
Code review policy: PRs require 2 approvals before merge. Self-merge on main is prohibited. Main branch is protected. Feature flags required for all risky deployments.
